title: Relate Multiplication and Division
essential_question: How are multiplication and division related?
strategies: Use a multiplication fact to solve a division problem.
Multiplication and division are inverse operations — one undoes the other.

learn.title: Inverse Operations
learn.content: Multiplication and division are inverse (opposite) operations. If 5 × 9 = 45, then 45 ÷ 5 = 9 and 45 ÷ 9 = 5. They undo each other.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.6 Lesson 6.7 'Relate Multiplication and Division' pp.245-248
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic E — Division as Unknown-Factor Problem
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Think Multiplication to Divide
learn.content: To divide, think of the related multiplication. For 54 ÷ 6: think '? × 6 = 54.' Since 9 × 6 = 54, then 54 ÷ 6 = 9. The multiplication fact is your key!

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.6 Lesson 6.7 'Relate Multiplication and Division' pp.245-248
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic E — Division as Unknown-Factor Problem
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Division as Unknown Factor
learn.content: 32 ÷ 8 = ? is the same as asking '? × 8 = 32.' The answer is 4, because 4 × 8 = 32. Think of division as finding the missing factor.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.6 Lesson 6.7 'Relate Multiplication and Division' pp.245-248
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic E — Division as Unknown-Factor Problem
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: Fact Family Triangle
learn.content: A fact family uses 3 numbers to make 4 equations. For 4, 7, and 28: 4 × 7 = 28, 7 × 4 = 28, 28 ÷ 4 = 7, 28 ÷ 7 = 4. All four facts live in the same family!

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.6 Lesson 6.7 'Relate Multiplication and Division' pp.245-248
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic E — Division as Unknown-Factor Problem
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: The Inverse Rule
learn.content: The rule: a ÷ b = ? is always the same as ? × b = a. Division is the 'undo' of multiplication. So if you know your multiplication facts, you can solve any division!

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.6 Lesson 6.7 'Relate Multiplication and Division' pp.245-248
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic E — Division as Unknown-Factor Problem
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 곱셈으로 나눗셈 풀기 — 사고 절차
learn.content: 나눗셈 문제를 만나면 무조건 곱셈으로 바꿔 생각하세요. 예: 56 ÷ 8 = ? → '몇 × 8 = 56?' → 7 → 답 7. 예: 42 ÷ 6 = ? → '몇 × 6 = 42?' → 7 → 답 7. 단계 1) 나눗셈 식을 본다 (피제수 ÷ 제수). 단계 2) 곱셈 미지수 식으로 바꾼다 (? × 제수 = 피제수). 단계 3) 곱셈표로 ?를 찾는다. 흔한 실수 (M06): 곱셈 사실을 외워놓고도 나눗셈 풀 때 다시 그림 그림.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.6 Lesson 6.7 'Relate Multiplication and Division' pp.245-248
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic E — Division as Unknown-Factor Problem
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 패밀리 4식 — 한 사실로 4가지
learn.content: 곱셈 1개를 알면 4식이 모두 풀림. 예: 7 × 8 = 56을 알면 자동으로:   7 × 8 = 56 (곱셈 1)   8 × 7 = 56 (곱셈 2 — 교환법칙)   56 ÷ 7 = 8 (나눗셈 1 — 역연산)   56 ÷ 8 = 7 (나눗셈 2 — 역연산) 패밀리 삼각형: 위 56, 아래 7과 8 → 한 모서리 가리면 그 자리가 답.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.6 Lesson 6.7 'Relate Multiplication and Division' pp.245-248
verification.procedure_source: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 1 Topic E — Division as Unknown-Factor Problem
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

learn.title: 미지수 인수 전략 — 시각적 흐름
learn.content: 나눗셈 a ÷ b = ? 를 풀 때:   ? × b = a 로 다시 쓰기 → b의 곱셈표에서 a를 찾기 → ?는 그 자리 행/열 수. 예: 36 ÷ 4 → ? × 4 = 36 → 4의 곱셈표에서 36 → 9번째 → ? = 9. 🔍 검증: 9 × 4 = 36 ✓. 팁: 곱셈표를 머릿속에 그림으로 두면 모든 나눗셈을 1초만에 해결.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.6 Lesson 6.7 'Relate Multiplication and Division' pp.245-248

hints: 48 plants in 6 rows → 48 ÷ 6 = ? per row. Find the inverse multiplication.
6 × 8 = 48 is the related multiplication: 48 ÷ 6 = 8 plants per row.

feedback.correct: Correct! 6 × 8 = 48 is the related multiplication for 48 ÷ 6 = 8.
feedback.incorrect: Not quite. 48 ÷ 6 = 8 plants per row → the multiplication is 6 × 8 = 48.

practice_r2.question: A garden has 48 plants in 6 rows. Which multiplication fact matches?
practice_r2.feedback_correct: Correct! 6 × 8 = 48 is the related multiplication for 48 ÷ 6 = 8.

choices: Yes — but only if you draw an array
practice_r3.explanation: Both division facts come from 5 × 6 = 30: 30 ÷ 5 = 6 and 30 ÷ 6 = 5.
hints: One multiplication fact gives TWO division facts — divide by either factor.

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. One multiplication always gives two related divisions: 30 ÷ 5 = 6 and 30 ÷ 6 = 5.

hints: Step 1: find pencils per box. Think: 8 × ? = 72 → 72 ÷ 8 = ?

feedback.incorrect: Not quite. 72 ÷ 8 = 9 pencils per box. Then 9 − 3 = 6 remain.

practice_r3.question: Mrs. Lee has 72 pencils. She divides them equally into 8 boxes. Then she gives away 3 pencils from each box. How many pencils remain in each box?

hints: Step 1: find the number. ? × 9 = 81 → think: 9 × 9 = 81 → number = 9.

feedback.correct: Correct! 9 × 9 = 81, so the number is 9. Then 9 ÷ 3 = 3.

practice_r3.question: A number times 9 equals 81. Divide that number by 3. What is the result?
practice_r3.feedback_correct: Correct! 9 × 9 = 81, so the number is 9. Then 9 ÷ 3 = 3.
